---
title: "Beta Diversity Analysis using Bray-Curtis"
author: Yanting Xiong
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-15"
---

## Library loading

```{r libraries, include = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
```

## Dataset loading and wrangling

```{r load_data}
# Load in the filtered and wrangled Parkinson's metadata
parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat = read.csv('../../../Datasets/Metadata/parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat.csv', row.names = 1)

# Because we will be controlling for entacapone in downstream analyses and it contains NA values, they need to be removed from the filtered metadata
parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat_entacapone_NA_removed = parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat %>% filter(!is.na(entacapone))
# 10 PD individuals are removed after this filtering step because they do not have entacapone intakes recorded

# Re-level the categorical variable in the metadata so that it appears in the order “low,” “medium,” and “high” in the plot, rather than the default alphabetical order
vars = c("Tert_BC", "Tert_AC", "Tert_VA", "Tert_VC", "Tert_VE", "Tert_combine") # store the column names

prefix = c("BC", "AC", "VA", "VC", "VE", "Combine") # stores the label prefixes used in the factor levels

for (i in seq_along(vars)) {
  parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat_entacapone_NA_removed[[vars[i]]] <- fct_relevel(
    parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat_entacapone_NA_removed[[vars[i]]],
    paste(prefix[i], "Low"),
    paste(prefix[i], "Medium"),
    paste(prefix[i], "High")
  )
}

# Load in feature table exported from QIIME2
counts = read.delim('../../../QIIME2_outputs/feature-table.txt', skip = 1, row.names = 1)

# Load in taxonomy table exported from QIIME2
taxonomy = read.delim('../../../QIIME2_outputs/taxonomy.tsv', row.names = 1)
```

## Make a Phyloseq object

```{r Create a Phyloseq object}
taxonomy_formatted = taxonomy %>% 
  separate(col = Taxon,
           into = c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family",
                    "Genus", "Species"),
           sep = ";", fill = "right") %>% 
  select(-Confidence) %>% 
  as.matrix()

counts_formatted = counts %>% as.matrix()

ps = phyloseq(sample_data(parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat_entacapone_NA_removed),
              otu_table(counts_formatted, taxa_are_rows = T),
              tax_table(taxonomy_formatted))
```

## Alpha rarefaction curve

```{r alpha_rarefaction}
# Alpha rarefaction curve
rarecurve(t(data.frame(ps@otu_table)),
          step = 1000)

# Based on the alpha rarefaction curve and the PD-filtered-table.qzv results, a sampling depth of 6515 will be chosen because at this sampling depth, only 14 samples are excluded from the analysis and we have retained 1,192,428 (50.04%) features in 183 (92.89%) samples at this specified sampling depth.

# Rarefy the data based on the rarefaction depth chosen
set.seed(421)
psrare = ps %>% rarefy_even_depth(sample.size = 6515, rngseed = 421)
```

## Beta Diversity Analysis

### Pre-processing

```{r calculate beta-curtis distances}
ps_bray = phyloseq::distance(psrare, method = "bray")
# View(as.matrix(ps_bray)) # Every pairwise comparison included

# MDS scaling
set.seed(421)
mds = metaMDS(ps_bray)

# Extract data
mds_data = mds$points %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
  merge(sample_data(psrare), by = "row.names", sort = F)
head(mds_data)
```

### Beta carotene

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_beta_carotene}
# Plot it
p_BC = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_BC)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Beta-carotene Intake Categorization") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("BC Low" = "steelblue", "BC Medium" = "goldenrod2", "BC High" = "indianred3"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_BC

parkinsons_metadata = sample_data(psrare) %>% data.frame()

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_BC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_BC, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_BC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_BC + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_BC = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_BC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_BC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_BC = stats_BC %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines

```

#### "BC Low" vs. "BC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_BC_LH = c("BC Low", "BC High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_BC_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_BC_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_BC_LH, samples_to_keep_BC_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_BC_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_BC_LH ~ Tert_BC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_LH)) 
stats_multivar_BC_LH # p-value is 0.003

```

#### "BC Low" vs. "BC Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_BC_LM = c("BC Low", "BC Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_BC_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_BC_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_BC_LM, samples_to_keep_BC_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_BC_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_BC_LM ~ Tert_BC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_LM)) 
stats_multivar_BC_LM # p-value is 0.296

```

#### "BC Medium" vs. "BC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (medium and high)
to_compare_BC_MH = c("BC Medium", "BC High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_BC_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_BC_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_BC_MH, samples_to_keep_BC_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_BC_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_BC_MH ~ Tert_BC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_BC %in% to_compare_BC_MH)) 
stats_multivar_BC_MH # p-value is 0.002

```


### Alpha carotene

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_alpha_carotene}
# Plot it
p_AC = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_AC)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Alpha-carotene Intake Categorization") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("AC Low" = "steelblue", "AC Medium" = "goldenrod2", "AC High" = "indianred3"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_AC

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_AC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_AC, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_AC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_AC + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_AC = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_AC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_AC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_AC = stats_AC %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines
```

#### "AC Low" vs. "AC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_AC_LH = c("AC Low", "AC High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest
samples_to_keep_AC_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_AC_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_AC_LH, samples_to_keep_AC_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_AC_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_AC_LH ~ Tert_AC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_LH)) 
stats_multivar_AC_LH #p-value is 0.129 
```


#### "AC Low" vs. "AC Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_AC_LM = c("AC Low", "AC Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_AC_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_AC_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_AC_LM, samples_to_keep_AC_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_AC_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_AC_LM ~ Tert_AC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_LM)) 
stats_multivar_AC_LM #p-value is 0.072
```
#### "AC Medium" vs. "AC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (medium and high)
to_compare_AC_MH = c("AC Medium", "AC High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_AC_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_AC_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_AC_MH, samples_to_keep_AC_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_AC_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_AC_MH ~ Tert_AC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_AC %in% to_compare_AC_MH)) 
stats_multivar_AC_MH #p-value is 0.052
```

### Vitamin C

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_vitamin_c}
# Plot it
p_VC = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_VC)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Vitamin C Intake Categorization") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("VC Low" = "steelblue", "VC Medium" = "goldenrod2", "VC High" = "indianred3"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_VC

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_VC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VC, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VC = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VC + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_VC = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_VC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_VC %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_VC = stats_VC %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines
```

#### "VC Low" vs. "VC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_VC_LH = c("VC Low", "VC High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest
samples_to_keep_VC_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VC_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VC_LH, samples_to_keep_VC_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VC_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VC_LH ~ Tert_VC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_LH)) 
stats_multivar_VC_LH # p-value is 0.006

```

#### "VC Low" vs. "VC Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_VC_LM = c("VC Low", "VC Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_VC_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VC_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VC_LM, samples_to_keep_VC_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VC_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VC_LM ~ Tert_VC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_LM))
stats_multivar_VC_LM # p-value is 0.258

```

#### "VC Medium" vs. "VC High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (medium and high)
to_compare_VC_MH = c("VC High", "VC Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_VC_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VC_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VC_MH, samples_to_keep_VC_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VC_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VC_MH ~ Tert_VC + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% to_compare_VC_MH)) 
stats_multivar_VC_MH # p-value is 0.002

```

### Vitamin A

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_vitamin_a}
# Plot it
p_VA = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_VA)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Vitamin A Intake Categorization") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("VA Low" = "steelblue", "VA Medium" = "goldenrod2", "VA High" = "indianred3", title = "Beta Diversity Analysis on Varying Levels of Vitamin A Intake"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_VA

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_VA = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VA, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VA = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VA + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_VA = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_VA %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_VA %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_VA = stats_VA %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines
```

#### "VA Low vs. "VA High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_VA_LH = c("VA Low", "VA High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_VA_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VA_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VA_LH, samples_to_keep_VA_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VA_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VA_LH ~ Tert_VA + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_LH)) 
stats_multivar_VA_LH # p-value is 0.06

```

#### "VA Low vs. "VA Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_VA_LM = c("VA Low", "VA Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest (low and high)
samples_to_keep_VA_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VA_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VA_LM, samples_to_keep_VA_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VA_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VA_LM ~ Tert_VA + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_LM)) 
stats_multivar_VA_LM # p-value is 0.156

```
#### "VA Medium vs. "VA High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (medium and high)
to_compare_VA_MH = c("VA High", "VA Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest (low and high)
samples_to_keep_VA_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VA_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VA_MH, samples_to_keep_VA_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VA_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VA_MH ~ Tert_VA + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% to_compare_VA_MH)) 
stats_multivar_VA_MH # p-value is 0.009

```

### Vitamin E

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_vitamin_e}
# Plot it
p_VE = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_VE)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Vitamin E Intake Categorization") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("VE Low" = "steelblue", "VE Medium" = "goldenrod2", "VE High" = "indianred3"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_VE

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_VE = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VE, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VE = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_VE + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_VE = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_VE %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_VE %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                       rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                     .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_VE = stats_VE %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines
```

#### "VE Low" vs "VE High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_VE_LH = c("VE Low", "VE High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest
samples_to_keep_VE_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VE_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VE_LH, samples_to_keep_VE_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VE_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VE_LH ~ Tert_VE + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_LH)) 
stats_multivar_VE_LH # p-value is 0.001

```

#### "VE Low" vs "VE Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_VE_LM = c("VE Low", "VE Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest
samples_to_keep_VE_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VE_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VE_LM, samples_to_keep_VE_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VE_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VE_LM ~ Tert_VE + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_LM))
stats_multivar_VE_LM # p-value is 0.009

```

#### "VE High" vs "VE Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_VE_MH = c("VE High", "VE Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest
samples_to_keep_VE_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_VE_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_VE_MH, samples_to_keep_VE_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_VE_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_VE_MH ~ Tert_VE + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                               data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_VE %in% to_compare_VE_MH)) 
stats_multivar_VE_MH # p-value is 0.009

```

### Five dietary antioxidants combined together

```{r beta_diversity_analysis_for_combined_dietary_antioxidants}
# Plot it
p_combine = mds_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(MDS1, MDS2, color = Tert_combine)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_ellipse() + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  labs(color = "Categorization of Combined Dietary Antioxidant Intake") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Combine Low" = "steelblue", "Combine Medium" = "goldenrod2", "Combine High" = "indianred3"),
    labels = c("Low intake", "Medium intake", "High intake")
  )
p_combine

# PERMANOVA test
# Single variable
set.seed(421)
stats_univar_combine = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_combine, data = parkinsons_metadata)

# Factor in confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_combine = adonis2(ps_bray ~ Tert_combine + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = parkinsons_metadata, by = "margin")

# Combine into a table
stats_combine = bind_rows("Univariate" = stats_univar_combine %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
                            rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                          "Multivariate" = stats_multivar_combine %>% as.data.frame() %>%
                            rownames_to_column("Variable"),
                          .id = "Model") # first column is Model, either Uni or Multi

stats_combine = stats_combine %>% rename(Pval = `Pr(>F)`) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Pval)) # remove irrelevant lines
```

#### "Combine Low" vs. "Combine High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and high)
to_compare_combine_LH = c("Combine Low", "Combine High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_combine_LH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_LH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_combine_LH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_combine_LH, samples_to_keep_combine_LH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_combine_LH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_combine_LH ~ Tert_combine + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                                    data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_LH))
stats_multivar_combine_LH # p-value is 0.001
```

#### "Combine Low" vs. "Combine Medium"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (low and medium)
to_compare_combine_LM = c("Combine Low", "Combine Medium")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_combine_LM = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_LM) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_combine_LM = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_combine_LM, samples_to_keep_combine_LM] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_combine_LM = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_combine_LM ~ Tert_combine + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                                    data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_LM))
stats_multivar_combine_LM # p-value is 0.172 
```

#### "Combine Medium" vs. "Combine High"

```{r}
# List samples that belong to the categories of interest (medium and high)
to_compare_combine_MH = c("Combine Medium", "Combine High")

# List samples that belong to these categories of interest 
samples_to_keep_combine_MH = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_MH) %>%
  rownames()

# Subset the distance matrix to only include those two categories of interest
ps_bray_sub_combine_MH = as.matrix(ps_bray)[samples_to_keep_combine_MH, samples_to_keep_combine_MH] %>%
  as.dist()

# Run a PERMANOVA test again and account for the real confounding variables
set.seed(421)
stats_multivar_combine_MH = adonis2(ps_bray_sub_combine_MH ~ Tert_combine + Age + entacapone + Sex,
                                    data = parkinsons_metadata %>% filter(Tert_combine %in% to_compare_combine_MH))
stats_multivar_combine_MH # p-value is 0.003
```
